title: Mock Server (MSW)
description: A tool for intercepting network requests at the browser level. Allows frontend development independent of backend readiness using typed mocks.
benefit_text: Allows testing <one>error scenarios</one>, network delays, and various data states without a real backend.

handlers.label: Handlers:
handlers.text: Located in <one>src/entities/[entity-name]/handlers</one>/. Files named <two>[entity-name].handlers.ts</two>. Contain logic for intercepting entity-specific requests.

mocks.label: Mocks:
mocks.text: Located in <one>src/entities/[entity-name]/mock</one>/. Files named <two>[entity-name].mock.ts</two>. Contain static or dynamic data simulating a server response.

config.label: Configuration:
config.text: General MSW settings are in <one>src/shared/api/msw</one>/. All handlers are registered in <two>handlers.ts</two>.

mocks.title: 1. Creating Mocks
mocks.description: Define a data array to be returned by the server. Use interfaces from the <one>types</one>/ folder for typing.

handlers.title: 2. Defining Handlers
handlers.description: Use the <one>msw</one> library to intercept specific URLs. You can also simulate network delay or server errors here.

init_browser.title: 3. Worker Configuration
init_browser.description: Create a worker instance in <one>shared/api/msw/browser.ts</one> to intercept browser requests.

init_handlers.title: 4. Handlers Registry
init_handlers.description: Assemble handlers from all entities into a single array in <one>shared/api/msw/handlers.ts</one>.

init_app.title: 5. App Initialization
init_app.description: Create an async startup function in <one>app/init/msw.ts</one> that checks environment flags.

init_main.title: 6. Entry Point (Main)
init_main.description: Wait for MSW to start before rendering the application in <one>src/main.tsx</one>.

env.title: 7. Environment Variables
env.description: Managing MSW activation via the <one>.env</one> file.
